name: Seven Hanging Valleys Trail
region: Lagoa e Carvoeiro e Carvoeiro e Carvoeiro
description: Hike the Sete Vales Suspensos circular route for stunning Algarve coastal views, dramatic cliffs, and picturesque beaches like Marinha.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Lagoa e Carvoeiro are characterized by dramatic coastal landscapes, unique rock formations, and scenic paths along the Atlantic Ocean. The region features rugged limestone cliffs, secluded beaches, and a network of trails that offer diverse views. These paths often lead through areas with grottoes, natural arches, and viewpoints overlooking the sea. Best family friendly hiking trails around Lagoa e Carvoeiro The most popular family friendly hiking route is Seven Hanging Valleys Trail, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 4 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views of the coastline and its distinctive geological features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Benagil Caves – Benagil Cliffs and Beach loop from Praia da Marinha, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This trail provides views of the famous Benagil Cave from above and passes by Praia da Marinha. Local hikers also love the Benagil Caves – View of the Algarve cliffs loop from Praia de Benagil, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through coastal cliffs and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Lagoa e Carvoeiro is defined by coastal paths, limestone cliffs, and unique rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, including many family friendly routes. The routes in Lagoa e Carvoeiro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4800 reviews. More than 30000 hikers have used komoot to explore Lagoa e Carvoeiro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: There are over 25 easy-going family-friendly hiking trails and walks around Lagoa e Carvoeiro. These routes are specifically chosen for their manageable distances and gentle paths, making them perfect for families with children.

Q: What kind of landscapes can we expect on family hikes in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: Family hikes in Lagoa e Carvoeiro offer a captivating blend of dramatic coastal landscapes, unique rock formations, and scenic trails. You'll encounter rugged limestone cliffs, secluded beaches, and the turquoise Atlantic Ocean. The area is renowned for its natural arches, sea caves (viewable from above), and highly weathered landscapes like Algar Seco.

Q: Are there any easy circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, there are several family-friendly circular routes. For a pleasant loop with stunning coastal views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32251661" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontainhas Beach – Pontal Beach loop from Forte e Capela de Nossa Senhora da Rocha&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 4.6 km long and features gentle elevation changes.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see along family trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Along the trails, families can discover the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/165055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Seco Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt; with their gnarled cliffs and natural pools, or marvel at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Arch at Praia da Marinha&lt;/a&gt;. The famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/867457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benagil Caves&lt;/a&gt; can also be viewed from above on certain coastal paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for family hiking in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: The Algarve region generally offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are ideal, with comfortable temperatures and fewer crowds. Winter can also be lovely for walks, offering mild weather, while summer can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the famous Benagil Cave?
A: While the Benagil Cave itself is only accessible by water, you can enjoy spectacular views of the coastline above it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17224452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benagil Caves – Benagil Cliffs and Beach loop from Praia da Marinha&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent vantage points to appreciate the dramatic cliffs and the area surrounding the cave.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about family-friendly trails in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: The family-friendly trails in Lagoa e Carvoeiro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.46 stars. Reviewers often praise the breathtaking coastal views, the unique rock formations, and the accessibility of many paths for various fitness levels, making them perfect for a family adventure.

Q: Are there any shorter, easy walks suitable for very young children or strollers?
A: Many of the trails in the region feature boardwalks and relatively flat sections, particularly around popular areas like Algar Seco, which can be suitable for younger children and some strollers. While not all trails are fully stroller-friendly due to uneven sections, there are accessible paths that allow families to enjoy the coastal scenery.

Q: Can we find trails that lead to beautiful beaches?
A: Absolutely! Many trails in Lagoa e Carvoeiro lead to or offer stunning views of beautiful beaches. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421830157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seven Hanging Valleys Trail&lt;/a&gt; connects several magnificent beaches, including Praia da Marinha and Praia de Vale Centeanes, both known for their golden cliffs and clear waters.

Q: Are there any trails that feature lighthouses or historical points of interest?
A: Yes, you can incorporate historical points into your family hike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2961533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Farol de Alfanzina&lt;/a&gt; (Alfanzina Lighthouse) is a notable landmark offering breathtaking coastal views and is often part of longer coastal walks. Additionally, the Chapel of Nossa Senhora da Rocha, situated on a narrow cliff, provides superb ocean viewpoints.

Q: Are there any trails with interesting rock formations to explore?
A: The region is famous for its unique geological formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32208369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Algarve cliffs – Albandeira Arch loop from Praia da Marinha&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that allows you to discover the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1735112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albandeira Arch&lt;/a&gt; and other fascinating cliff structures.
tips: Unique beach with exciting rock formations

This well-marked hiking trail between Praia de Marinha and Praia de Vale Centeaes is popular for its breathtaking views and the beautiful rock formations that hang over the water. The path is easy for anyone with a reasonable level of fitness, although there is some scrambling here and there. You can start the walk at different points, but a nice starting point is Praia Marinha. It is best to go to the equally breathtaking caves by boat (or if you are a very good swimmer)

The Alfanzina Lighthouse built in 1920 on a rocky promontory. It is a white quadrangular tower with a red lantern, important for navigation. The lighthouse is accessible to the public on Wednesdays. Its facilities were expanded in 1952 and connected to the electricity grid in 1980.

Praia do Vale de Centeanes, located near Carvoeiro, is famous for its natural beauty and proximity to the Benagil cave and is accessible to people with reduced mobility. The beach is sheltered by golden cliffs, offering a peaceful environment. It is also the starting point of the Seven Hanging Valleys trail. The beach is guarded, ideal for families and has a restaurant with sea views.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Sete Vales Suspensos trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be started from either end. Popular starting points with parking include Praia da Marinha or Praia do Vale Centeanes. You can also find parking near Benagil or Praia do Carvalho, allowing you to customize your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Sete Vales Suspensos trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on the trail, it's important to keep them on a leash due to the clifftop nature and uneven terrain. Some beaches along the route may have restrictions, especially during peak season, so check local signage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Seven Hanging Valleys trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is during spring (March to May) when wildflowers bloom and temperatures are mild, or in autumn (September to November) after the summer heat subsides. Summer can be very hot, making an early start essential, and winter can bring rain, making some sections slippery.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the Sete Vales Suspensos trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this coastal route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily a clifftop walk, characterized by rugged limestone cliffs. You'll encounter rough, uneven, rocky, and occasionally slippery paths, especially after rain. There are also occasional steep climbs and descents. The path includes natural and gravel sections, with smaller portions of unpaved, paved, and asphalt surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers spectacular views and unique geological features. Highlights include the stunning Marinha Beach, the famous Benagil Caves (viewable from above), the Farol de Alfanzina lighthouse, and the picturesque Vale de Centeanes Beach. You'll also see numerous sea stacks, arches, and natural wells carved into the cliffs.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, which is approximately 11.5 km (7.1 miles) long, typically takes around 3 hours to complete. This duration can vary depending on your pace, how many stops you make for photos, and whether you spend time exploring the beaches.

faqs.question: Is the Sete Vales Suspensos trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While generally considered of moderate difficulty, the trail's uneven, rocky terrain and occasional steep sections mean good fitness and sure-footedness are recommended. It might be challenging for very young children or those unaccustomed to hiking. Sturdy hiking shoes are essential for everyone.

faqs.question: What should I pack or wear for the hike?
faqs.answer: Sturdy hiking shoes with good grip are essential due to the rocky and uneven terrain. Bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months, as there are limited facilities directly on the trail. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also crucial, as much of the route is exposed. A camera is a must for the incredible views!

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is mostly natural, you'll find cafes and restaurants near the main starting points and beaches like Praia da Marinha, Benagil, and Praia do Vale Centeanes. These are good spots to refuel before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Are there any tips for making the most of the hike?
faqs.answer: To avoid the intense summer sun and crowds, start your hike early in the morning. Always stick to the marked paths and avoid cliff edges, as some overhangs can be precarious. Consider bringing binoculars for spotting marine life or distant rock formations.
